description: Phase 13 — Full Map View. Dedicated /map route with MapLibre plotting every beach as a colour-coded, score-labelled marker. Clustering at low zoom, bottom-sheet previews on tap, persistent camera state, locate-me control, offline banner, and a toggleable wind-arrow overlay. Symmetric Map ⇄ List FAB. No filter integration, no heatmap, no tide overlay, no tile precaching.

userStories.title: Add /map route and symmetric Map ⇄ List FAB
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single FAB that toggles between list and map so switching views feels like one obvious gesture in both directions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add route to ui/src/router.ts: { path: '/map', name: 'beach-map', component: () => import('./views/MapView.vue') }

Create ui/src/views/MapView.vue with a placeholder

Map

(real map comes in US-002)
Create ui/src/components/ViewSwitcherFab.vue: fixed bottom-right, 56px circle, background var(--color-ocean-800), white icon, honours env(safe-area-inset-bottom) in bottom offset

FAB reads the current route; on '/' shows lucide 'map' icon with aria-label 'Open map view' and navigates to { name: 'beach-map' }; on '/map' shows lucide 'list' icon with aria-label 'Open list view' and navigates to '/'
Register 'map' and 'list' lucide icons in ui/src/components/lit/surf-icon.ts if missing, following the existing heart/download/sliders-horizontal/cloud-off pattern
Mount ViewSwitcherFab in both HomeView.vue and MapView.vue (or once at app-shell level — whichever is simpler; must render on both routes)
On HomeView, hide the FAB while beachStore.loading is true OR beachStore.error is set; on MapView the FAB is always shown
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tap FAB on home, map route opens; tap FAB on map, list route returns

userStories.title: Render MapLibre map with all beaches and fit-to-bounds on first visit
userStories.description: As a user opening the map for the first time, I want to see every beach on a map zoomed to fit them all so I get an overview without panning.
acceptanceCriteria: MapView.vue calls beachStore.fetchBeaches() on mount if beachStore.beaches.length === 0
Initialise a MapLibre map filling the viewport (container has height: 100vh, no persistent top header — the FAB from US-001 is the only nav affordance)
Use the same style URL as ui/src/components/BeachMap.vue: 'https://demotiles.maplibre.org/style.json'
On first visit (no persisted camera from US-005 yet), call map.fitBounds(bounds, { padding: 48 }) over every beach's [lon, lat]
Add a default MapLibre Marker for each beach (styling upgrade comes in US-003)
Clean up the map instance in onBeforeUnmount via map.remove()
Depends on US-001 (route must exist)
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: navigate to /map; confirm every beach appears as a marker and the map is zoomed to fit all beaches

userStories.title: Colour-coded circle markers with score number via GeoJSON source + circle/symbol layers
userStories.description: As a user scanning the map, I want each marker to show the beach's score inside a coloured circle so I can compare surfability visually without tapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the per-beach MapLibre Markers from US-002 with a GeoJSON source 'beaches' whose features carry properties: id (beach id), name, region, label (currentLabel or 'neutral'), score (currentScore or null), windSpeed, windDirection, lastUpdated

Add a circle layer 'beach-markers' with circle-radius 18, circle-stroke-width 2, circle-stroke-color '#ffffff', and circle-color driven by label via a match expression: 'very-good' → var(--color-surf-very-good), 'good' → var(--color-surf-good), 'maybe' → var(--color-surf-maybe), 'poor' → var(--color-surf-poor), default → var(--color-neutral-300). Resolve the CSS custom properties to hex in JS (read getComputedStyle on :root) and pass hex to paint

Add a symbol layer 'beach-marker-labels' on the same source: text-field is the score when score != null else '—'; text-size 13; text-color '#ffffff'; text-font set to a font listed in the demotiles style's glyphs (e.g. ['Noto Sans Regular']); text-allow-overlap true; text-ignore-placement true
Both layers filter OUT clusters (filter: ['!', ['has', 'point_count']]) — prep for clustering in US-004
A subtle drop shadow on the circle is acceptable via an additional circle layer beneath with larger radius and low opacity; not required
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: every beach marker is a coloured circle with its score inside; colours match the corresponding beach's badge on HomeView
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002. CSS custom properties cannot be passed directly to MapLibre paint — use getComputedStyle to resolve to hex at map init.

userStories.title: Cluster markers below zoom 8
userStories.description: As a user at country-level zoom, I want clustered markers so the map isn't overwhelmed with overlapping pins.
acceptanceCriteria: Update the 'beaches' GeoJSON source from US-003 with cluster: true, clusterMaxZoom: 8, clusterRadius: 50

Add a cluster-circle layer 'beach-clusters' filter ['has', 'point_count']: circle-color var(--color-ocean-700) resolved to hex; circle-stroke-color '#ffffff'; circle-stroke-width 2; circle-radius via step expression: ['step', ['get', 'point_count'], 18, 10, 22, 30, 28]
Add a cluster-count symbol layer 'beach-cluster-count' with filter ['has', 'point_count']: text-field ['get', 'point_count_abbreviated']; text-size 13; text-color '#ffffff'; text-font as in US-003
On click of 'beach-clusters' layer, call source.getClusterExpansionZoom(clusterId) and then map.easeTo({ center: clusterCenter, zoom: expansionZoom })
Individual marker layer (from US-003) continues to filter out clusters so no overlap
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: zoom out to a country-level view; confirm markers collapse into cluster bubbles with counts; tap a cluster and the map zooms in and the cluster expands

userStories.title: Persist map centre and zoom in IndexedDB settings
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want the map to open where I left it so I don't have to re-navigate to my area every time.
acceptanceCriteria: On MapView mount, read db.settings.get('mapCenter') and db.settings.get('mapZoom') before initialising the map
If both values exist and are valid, pass them as center and zoom in new maplibregl.Map({ ... }) and SKIP the fitBounds from US-002
If either is missing or invalid, fall back to the US-002 fit-to-bounds behaviour
On map 'moveend', write { key: 'mapCenter', value: [lng, lat] } and { key: 'mapZoom', value: zoom } to db.settings with a ~400ms debounce so rapid panning doesn't spam IndexedDB
No TypeScript 'any' — follow the existing typed read/write pattern used by filter persistence in ui/src/stores/beachStore.ts
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: pan and zoom, navigate away to '/', return to '/map'; camera matches where it was left

userStories.title: Bottom-sheet preview on marker tap
userStories.description: As a user tapping a marker, I want a quick preview card with the beach's key info and a button to open full details, without losing my place on the map.
acceptanceCriteria: Register a click handler on the 'beach-markers' (individual) layer from US-003 — clicks on clusters are handled separately in US-004
On click, look up the beach from beachStore.beaches by the feature's 'id' property
Open surf-bottom-sheet (import the existing ui/src/components/lit/surf-bottom-sheet.ts) with :open='true'

Sheet content shows: beach name (h2), region (subtitle), surf-badge with label and score (e.g. '72%'), swell/wind summary lines when the beach has swellHeight/swellPeriod/swellDirection/windSpeed/windDirection (reuse the degreesToCompass helper and hhmm helper patterns from HomeView), a staleness line built from lastUpdated via formatRelativeTime
Sheet footer contains a full-width surf-button variant='primary' labelled 'View details' that calls router.push({ name: 'beach-detail', params: { id: beach.id } })
Closing the sheet (backdrop tap / close gesture) returns focus to the map without navigation — use the sheet's existing close event
The bottom sheet already honours env(safe-area-inset-bottom) from Phase 12, so no change there
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: tap any marker; bottom sheet opens with name/region/score/conditions/View details; tap 'View details' navigates to the beach detail route
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003. Don't duplicate helpers — import or recreate the same computed pattern used in HomeView.

userStories.title: Locate-me control that pans to user geolocation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly centre the map on my current location to see beaches near me.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay a circular 'locate me' button at the top-right of the map, 44×44 px, background '#ffffff', border 1px solid var(--color-ocean-800), lucide 'locate-fixed' icon coloured var(--color-ocean-800)
Register 'locate-fixed' lucide icon in surf-icon.ts if missing
Button honours env(safe-area-inset-top) on its top offset and env(safe-area-inset-right) on its right offset
On click, call navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition with a 10s timeout
While awaiting a fix, set a local 'locating' reactive flag: button disabled via pointer-events: none and icon at opacity 0.5
On success, map.easeTo({ center: [lng, lat], zoom: Math.max(map.getZoom(), 11) })

On error or permission denial, render a small aria-live='polite' inline notice near the button (or reuse the offline-banner styling) with text 'Can't access your location' that auto-dismisses after 4s
Clear the 'locating' flag in both success and error paths
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: use DevTools → Sensors → override geolocation to a Greek coordinate; tap 'locate me' and confirm the map eases to that location at zoom ≥ 11

userStories.title: Offline notice when map opened without network
userStories.description: As a user opening the map offline, I want to be told the tiles won't load so I'm not left staring at a blank grid.
acceptanceCriteria: MapView.vue imports the existing ui/src/composables/useOnlineStatus.ts composable

When !isOnline on mount OR it flips false while viewing the map, render a full-width banner at the top of the map: 'Map needs internet — tiles won't load. Your cached beach data is still available on the list view.'

Banner uses background var(--color-surf-maybe) and white text, mirroring the existing offline-banner pattern in App.vue (do NOT duplicate App.vue's banner — the app-level banner still shows; this banner is the map-specific explanation with a CTA)
Banner includes a surf-button variant='secondary' labelled 'Back to list' that calls router.push('/')
Banner sits above the map and does NOT block interaction with the map itself — the map is still mounted underneath
Banner disappears automatically when isOnline becomes true
Banner honours env(safe-area-inset-top) in its top offset
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: DevTools Network → Offline, navigate to /map; banner appears; tap 'Back to list' returns to '/'
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002. Coexists with the app-level offline banner in App.vue.

userStories.title: Toggleable wind-arrow overlay
userStories.description: As a user comparing beaches on the map, I want to see wind direction and relative strength at each beach so I can spot offshore-wind spots without opening each detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: On map 'load', load a small arrow SVG (either bundled via Vite ESM import + URL.createObjectURL or inline data URL) and register it with map.addImage('arrow', image) — the arrow points up by default so icon-rotate rotates around the marker centre
Add a symbol layer 'beach-wind-arrows' on the existing 'beaches' GeoJSON source with filter ['!', ['has', 'point_count']] (never render arrows on clusters)

Layer paint/layout: icon-image 'arrow'; icon-rotate ['get', 'windDirection']; icon-size ['interpolate', ['linear'], ['get', 'windSpeed'], 0, 0.5, 30, 1.0]; icon-opacity 0.85; icon-allow-overlap true; icon-ignore-placement true; icon-offset [0, -28] so arrows sit above the score circle

Features missing windSpeed or windDirection produce null icon-rotate/icon-size; use a second filter that requires both: ['all', ['!', ['has', 'point_count']], ['has', 'windSpeed'], ['has', 'windDirection']]

Overlay a circular toggle button at the top-right of the map, 44×44 px, positioned BELOW the locate-me button (stacked with ~8px gap), using lucide 'wind' icon — register the icon in surf-icon.ts if missing
Active state: background var(--color-ocean-800), icon white; inactive state: background '#ffffff', border 1px solid var(--color-ocean-800), icon var(--color-ocean-800)
Toggling flips map.setLayoutProperty('beach-wind-arrows', 'visibility', 'visible' | 'none') — no re-render
Persist the toggle state in db.settings under key 'mapWindOverlay' (boolean); read it on map mount to restore the initial visibility (default false on first visit)
Safe-area insets honoured on the button's top-right offsets
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: toggle on — arrows appear rotated per beach and sized by wind speed; toggle off — arrows disappear; reload the page with toggle on — arrows are visible on mount
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (GeoJSON source) and US-007 (locate-me button position — stack under it). Wind-direction convention assumed: degrees = bearing wind is coming FROM; document this next to the icon registration.
